_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 9-28R Ipratropium Bromide (Revised 8/11/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template.
protocol_reference: Michigan Medication Protocol Section 9-28R: Ipratropium Bromide — MDHHS Approved 8/11/2023, Reviewed 2023
indications: Respiratory distress with bronchospasm not fully responsive to albuterol
contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to atropine or atropine derivatives (ipratropium is structurally related to atropine)
precautions: Used as second-line bronchodilator after albuterol — not first-line
May combine with albuterol in same nebulizer as 'DuoNeb' combination treatment
Systemic anticholinergic effects are minimal with nebulized route at standard doses
expected_effects: Additive bronchodilation when combined with albuterol
side_effects: Blurred vision if medication contacts eyes (avoid eye exposure)
notes: Standard dose: 500 mcg (0.5 mg) in 2.5 mL NS nebulized
May combine with albuterol 2.5 mg/3 mL in same nebulizer cup as 'DuoNeb' combination
Administer after albuterol for continued wheezing — not as first-line treatment

dosing.notes: Administer after albuterol for continued wheezing; may combine with albuterol 2.5 mg/3 mL as DuoNeb
